title: Carex campylorhina
intro: Carex campylorhina, also known as the twisted-stalk sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Carex campylorhina include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the twisted-stalk sedge is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Carex campylorhina is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. It belongs to the genus Carex, which consists of approximately 2,000 species of sedges. As a member of the Carex genus, Carex campylorhina is further categorized into the subfamily Caricoideae. This classification helps in understanding the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other sedges. The classification of Carex campylorhina is a crucial aspect in plant systematics and taxonomy.
